title: Jabed Meeker, Humorist
content:
آ آ آ آ Twain? Oh, yes, Iï؟½ve heard Mark Twain
آ آ آ آ Heard him down to Pleasant Plain;
آ آ آ آ Funny? Yes, I guess so. Folks
آ آ آ آ Seemed to laugh loud at his jokes,
آ آ آ آ Laughed to beat the band; but I
آ آ آ آ Couldnï؟½t rightly make out why.
آ آ آ آ Guess his humor ainï؟½t refined.
آ آ آ آ Quite enough to suit my mind.
آ آ آ آ Markï؟½s all right, right clever speaker,
آ آ آ آ But he canï؟½t touch Jabed Meeker;
آ آ آ آ And one thing that makes it queer
آ آ آ آ Is that Jabed lives right here.

آ آ آ آ You ainï؟½t met him? Son, youï؟½ve missed
آ آ آ آ The most funniest humorist
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve met with in my born days,
آ آ آ آ Funniest talker, anyways,
آ آ آ آ When it comes to repartee,
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s the humor catches me!

آ آ آ آ Like a specimen? Huh! Well,
آ آ آ آ Take, for instance, his umbrell;
آ آ آ آ Wouldnï؟½t think, until he spoke,
آ آ آ آ He could turn that to a joke;
آ آ آ آ Mark Twain couldnï؟½t, bet you that!
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s where Meeker beats Mark flat!

آ آ آ آ Just imagine three or four
آ آ آ آ Fellers in Jim Beemerï؟½s store,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Long comes Meeker, and some feller
آ آ آ آ Says, ï؟½See Meekerï؟½s bum umbreller.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Quick as lightning Meeker ï؟½d yell:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Donï؟½t you guy my bumberell!
آ آ آ آ Whereï؟½s the feller dares to hoot
آ آ آ آ At this sping-spang bumbershoot?
آ آ آ آ Show me some one dares to call
آ آ آ آ Bad names at my bumbersoll!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Right like that! Right off the reel!
آ آ آ آ Say, youï؟½d ought to heard us squeal!
آ آ آ آ Then, before weï؟½d got our breath,
آ آ آ آ Meeker, solemn sad as death,
آ آ آ آ Says: ï؟½Stand up there ï؟½gainst that wall,
آ آ آ آ Para-bumber-shooter-soll!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Twain? All right! But just give me
آ آ آ آ Some one slick at repartee!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ellis Parker Butler, titled "Jabed Meeker, Humorist", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
